blocks.text: Brackenridge Civil Group Inc is a civil engineering firm practicing site grading, stormwater, roadway, and utility design. Our clients are public works departments, water and sewer authorities, and private developers building in this county and the ones adjoining it. The firm is small by intention. There is no business development staff here. The engineer who signs the proposal is the engineer who does the work, and the engineer who takes the telephone call two years afterward when the contractor hits something nobody drew.

The scope of our practice has not changed since the firm opened and there is no plan to change it. We design what happens at grade: the movement of earth, the collection and conveyance of stormwater, the geometry of pavement, and the placement of pipe that has to fit beneath both. Work outside that scope is referred rather than attempted. Clients occasionally find that frustrating. They find it less frustrating than the alternative.

rows: Grading plans, cut and fill quantities, erosion and sediment control plans and narrative
Drainage area mapping, hydrologic and hydraulic calculations, conveyance and inlet design, detention and water quality facilities, permit narrative
Horizontal and vertical geometry, typical sections, intersection details, signing and striping, maintenance of traffic
Water distribution and sanitary collection plans, gravity and pressure profiles, utility conflict resolution
Bid documents, addenda, tabulation of bids, recommendation of award
Shop drawing review, responses to requests for information, periodic site observation, record drawings

blocks.text: Every sheet is produced in this office on our own CAD standard, checked against a print, and issued under the seal of the Principal Engineer. Nothing leaves the office that he has not read, and the schedule is not accepted as a reason to make an exception, because the schedule is always the reason offered.

The firm's continuing engagement is with {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}}, for whom this office has served as civil engineer of record on work of the kind tabulated above. That is the relationship we would ask a prospective client to check, and we will put you in touch on request rather than offer our own account of it.

The firm is also engaged by {{fact:f:E-2004-001.client}}. Both engagements are staffed out of this office. We do not broker work to a subconsultant and then administer it from here; if the drawing carries our seal, it was drawn in this room by someone whose name is on it.

We are aware that two clients is a short list. It is short because we have not chased volume, because we do not take work outside the scope described above, and because we do not count a proposal as a client. What we can tell a prospective client is that neither of the two has had occasion to go looking for another civil engineer.

Mark Todd, Principal Engineer, is responsible for the technical content of everything this firm issues and is the signatory on every agreement and every letter to a public body. Design and plan production are carried out by the firm's engineering staff, all of it in-house. The firm's project files are maintained by our administration in lettered project folders, and a client who telephones for a copy of an executed agreement or a transmittal from a closed job will have it the same day.

We propose against a defined scope, at a lump sum where the scope will support one and on an hourly basis where it will not, and we say which in the proposal rather than leave the client to discover it in the first invoice. Where we believe a scope is wrong, we say so before the agreement is executed. Additional services are proposed in writing and performed after authorization, never before it. We have not yet sent a client an invoice they had not already agreed to receive, and we intend to keep that record intact.

Correspondence and inquiries concerning the firm's availability or a specific project should be addressed to Mark Todd, Principal Engineer. We will contact you within the week. Proposals are prepared at no cost and are written to the scope described to us.
